author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XXIV

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A psalm of David himself, on the first day of the week.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A Psalm of David himself, touching the glorifying and resurrection of the Lord, which took place early in the morning on the first day of the week, which is now called the Lord’s Day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The earth is the Lord’s, and the fulness thereof, the compass of the world, and all they that dwell therein” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1); when the Lord, being glorified, is announced for the believing of all nations; and the whole compass of the world becomes His Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath founded it above the seas.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath most firmly established it above all the waves of this world, that they should be subdued by it, and should not hurt it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And hath prepared it above the rivers” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The rivers flow into the sea, and men of lust lapse into the world: these also the Church, which, when worldly lusts have been conquered by the grace of God, hath been prepared by love for the reception of immortality, subdues.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who shall ascend into the mount of the Lord?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who shall ascend to the height of the righteousness of the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Or who shall stand in His holy place?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or who shall abide in that place, whither He shall ascend, founded above the seas, and prepared above the rivers?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The innocent of hand, and the pure in heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who then shall ascend thither, and abide there, but the guiltless in deed, and pure in thought?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who hath not received his soul in vain.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who hath not reckoned his soul among things that pass away, but feeling it to be immortal, hath longed for an eternity stedfast and unchangeable.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And hath not sworn in deceit to his neighbour.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And therefore without deceit, as things eternal are simple and undeceiving, hath so behaved himself to his neighbour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “This man shall receive blessing from the Lord, and mercy from the God of his salvation” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “This is the generation of them that seek the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thus are they born that seek Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Of them that seek the face of the God of Jacob.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now they seek the face of God, who gave the pre-eminence to the younger born.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Take away your gates, ye princes” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All ye, that seek rule among men, remove, that they hinder not, the entrances which ye have made, of desire and fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And be ye lift up, ye everlasting gates.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And be ye lift up, ye entrances of eternal life, of renunciation of the world, and conversion to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the King of glory shall come in.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the King, in whom we may glory without pride, shall come in: who having overcome the gates of death, and having opened for Himself the heavenly places, fulfilled that which He said, “Be of good cheer, for I have overcome the world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who is this King of glory?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Mortal nature is awe-struck in wonder, and asks, “Who is this King of glory?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord strong and mighty.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He whom thou didst deem weak and overwhelmed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord mighty in battle” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Handle the scars, and thou wilt find them made whole, and human weakness restored to immortality.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The glorifying of the Lord, which was owing to earth, where It warred with death, hath been paid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Take away your gates, ye princes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us go hence straightway into heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again, let the Prophet’s trumpet cry aloud, “Take away too, ye princes of the air, the gates, which ye have in the minds of men who ‘worship the host of heaven.’”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And be ye lift up, ye everlasting gates.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And be ye lift up, ye doors of everlasting righteousness, of love, and chastity, through which the soul loveth the One True God, and goeth not a-whoring with the many that are called gods.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the King of glory shall come in” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the King of glory shall come in,” that He may at the right hand of the Father intercede for us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who is this King of glory?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What! dost thou too, prince of the power of this air, marvel and ask, “Who is this King of glory?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord of powers, He is the King of glory” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yea, His Body now quickened, He who was tempted marches above thee; He who was tempted by the angel, the deceiver, goes above all angels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let none of you put himself before us and stop our way, that he may be worshipped as a god by us: neither principality, nor angel, nor power, separateth us from the love of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is good to trust in the Lord, rather than to trust in a prince; that he who glorieth, should glory in the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These indeed are powers in the administration of this world, but “the Lord of powers, He is the King of glory.”
